segments.text: Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot in vain?
The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers gather together, against the LORD and against His Anointed One:
Let us break Their chains and cast away Their cords.
segments.speaker: Rebellious Kings

segments.text: The One enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord taunts them.
Then He rebukes them in His anger, and terrifies them in His fury:
I have installed My King on Zion, upon My holy mountain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will proclaim the decreespoken to Me by the LORD:
You are My Son; today I have become Your Father.
segments.speaker: The LORD

footnotes.text: + Literally today I have begotten You; cited in Acts 13:33, Hebrews 1:5, and Hebrews 5:5

segments.text: Ask Me, and I will make the nations Your inheritance, the ends of the earth Your possession.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You will break them with an iron scepter; You will shatter them like pottery.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes.text: + LXX You will rule them or You will shepherd them
+ Cited in Revelation 2:27; see also Revelation 12:5 and Revelation 19:15.

segments.text: Therefore be wise, O kings; be admonished, O judges of the earth.
Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.

Kiss the Son, lest He be angry and you perish in your rebellion,
when His wrath ignites in an instant.
Blessed are all who take refuge in Him.
